study_objective.primary_objective: Estimate the rate of pathologic complete response (pCR) in patients with triple‑negative breast cancer (TNBC) whose tumors express FASN, treated with standard neoadjuvant chemotherapy plus high‑dose omeprazole.
secondary_objectives: Quantify the number of newly diagnosed TNBC patients with tumors expressing FASN.
Estimate pCR rate in all TNBC patients (regardless of FASN status) treated with the combination regimen.
Describe the safety of adding high‑dose omeprazole to standard neoadjuvant chemotherapy.
Estimate biologic activity of high‑dose omeprazole in modulating FASN expression and activity.
exploratory_objectives: Compare peak omeprazole concentration to levels associated with preclinical activity (~5.6 μM).
Use PK‑PD models to relate omeprazole concentration and lipid changes to clinical outcomes.
Explore changes in lipid profile over time.

primary_endpoints: Pathologic complete response (pCR), defined as no invasive disease in the breast or axilla at surgery.
inclusion_criteria: Newly diagnosed TNBC, clinical stage I (T1c only), II, or III.
ER and PR < 10%; HER2 negative by IHC/FISH criteria.
Ability to provide informed consent and HIPAA authorization.
Negative pregnancy test within 14 days for women of childbearing potential.
Adequate organ function: LVEF > LLN; Hgb > 8.5; ANC > 1,000; platelets > 100,000; creatinine < 1.5; total bilirubin < 1.3; AST < 2.5× ULN.
exclusion_criteria: Use of prescription PPIs within 12 months (dexlansoprazole, pantoprazole, rabeprazole, esomeprazole, lansoprazole, omeprazole).
Use of OTC PPIs within 6 months.
Use of Orlistat or other known FASN inhibitors within 6 months.
Known hypersensitivity to PPI components or substituted benzimidazoles.

general: Dose modifications for AC and paclitaxel/carboplatin based on hematologic, hepatic, renal, cardiac, neurologic, and other toxicities.
Growth factor support recommended for dose‑dense AC.
omeprazole_specific: No planned dose reductions; discontinue if unable to take for >14 days.
Discontinue for acute interstitial nephritis, osteoporosis‑related fractures, vitamin B12 deficiency, C. difficile diarrhea, grade 3 hypomagnesemia, or any grade ≥3 toxicity attributed to omeprazole.
Potential CYP2C19 drug–drug interactions require monitoring.
chemotherapy_specific: Standard AC and paclitaxel toxicity management including neutropenia, thrombocytopenia, hepatic dysfunction, mucositis, neuropathy, and hypersensitivity reactions.
Carboplatin allowed but not recommended; requires additional renal and magnesium monitoring.
monitoring: Regular CBC, CMP, magnesium, B12, and cardiac function assessments.
Biopsy‑based monitoring of FASN expression and activity.

potential_regulatory_risks: Use of high‑dose omeprazole (off‑label for oncology) may require justification for safety and pharmacologic rationale.
Exclusion of patients with prior PPI exposure may limit generalizability and raise questions during IRB review.
Biopsy requirements (baseline and post‑omeprazole) introduce procedural risk and may require additional consent language.
Drug–drug interaction risks due to CYP2C19 inhibition may require enhanced safety monitoring and documentation.
Single‑arm design may be scrutinized for adequacy of efficacy interpretation and risk–benefit justification.
Protocol deviations related to chemotherapy dosing flexibility (allowed per institutional practice) may require careful regulatory documentation.
